zh.lifeSummary: 當Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich於1351年11月1日出生在奧地利維恩時，他的父親Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich52歲，母親Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt42歲。他於1365年2月23日與Viridis di Visconti在義大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾米蘭諾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1386年7月9日在瑞士盧爾謝帕賀享年34歲，埋葬在奧地利亞特沃爾福斯格聖 保羅 史蒂芬 拉望塔爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich于1351年11月1日出生在奥地利維恩时，他的父亲Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich52岁，母亲Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt42岁。他于1365年2月23日与Viridis di Visconti在意大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾米蘭諾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1386年7月9日在瑞士盧爾謝帕賀享年34岁，埋葬在奥地利亞特沃爾福斯格聖 保羅 史蒂芬 拉望塔爾。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Österreich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt, Uwe, Wolfgang.

German (rarely Österreich) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the province of Upper or Lower Austria, from German Österreich ‘Austria’, the name of which literally means ‘Eastern kingdom’.

variants.name: Oestreicher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Franz, Otto, Horst.  Jewish Shloime, Chaim, Mendy.
German (also Östreicher) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the province of Upper or Lower Austria (see Oesterreich ).

variants.name: Oestreich
meanings: German (also Östreich) and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Oesterreich .

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Runte  .

variants.name: Runte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erhard.

German: in the northwest of Germany most probably a topographic name from a field name based on run ‘mud’, which gave rise to several placenames in the Rhine-Ruhr area, for example Runten, Runtenberg, Rünthe near Hamm, Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: Leopold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from liut ‘people’ + bold ‘bold, brave’. The first element was altered by association with Latin leo ‘lion’. A name of this origin may have been introduced into Britain by the Normans, but if so it did not survive long. It was reintroduced to Britain from the Continent towards the end of the 19th‐century in honour of Leopold , King of the Belgians ( 1790–1865 ) and uncle of Queen Victoria , to whom he was an influential adviser in her youth: she named one of her sons after him.

nameMeanings.name: III.,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich was born on 1 November 1351, in Vienna, Austria, his father, Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich, was 52 and his mother, Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt, was 42. He married Viridis di Visconti on 23 February 1365, in Milano, Milan, Lombardy, Italy. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 9 July 1386, in Sempach, Lucerne, Switzerland, at the age of 34, and was buried in St. Paul im Lavanttal, Wolfsberg, Carinthia, Austria.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich est né le 1 novembre 1351, à Vienne, Autriche, son père, Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Viridis di Visconti le 23 février 1365, à Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Italie. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 9 juillet 1386, à Sempach, Lucerne, Suisse, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterré à St. Paul im Lavanttal, Wolfsberg, Kärnten, Autriche.

de.lifeSummary: Als Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich am 1. November 1351 in Wien, Österreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich 52 und seine Mutter Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt 42. Er hat am 23. Februar 1365 in Milano, Milano, Lombardei, Italien Viridis di Visconti geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 9. Juli 1386 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Sempach, Luzern, Schweiz und wurde in St. Paul im Lavanttal, Wolfsberg, Kärnten, Österreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich è nato il il 1 novembre 1351 nel Wien, Austria, il suo padre, Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich, aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt, aveva 42 anni. Lui sposato Viridis di Visconti il 23 febbraio 1365, nel Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Italia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 9 luglio 1386, nel Sempach, Lucerna, Svizzera, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolto a St. Paul im Lavanttal, Wolfsberg, Carinzia, Austria.

ja.lifeSummary: Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreichが1351年11月1日にオーストリアウィンで生まれた時，父Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreichは52歳，母Johanna Gräfin von Pfirtは42歳であった。1365年2月23日にイタリアロンバーディアミラノミラノでViridis di Viscontiと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1386年7月9日にスイスルズエルンセンプアチで34歳で死去し，オーストリアクaルンテンウオルフスバーグスト. プアウル イム ラヴァンッタルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich(이)가 1351년 11월 1일 오스트리아 빈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1365년 2월 23일 이탈리아 롬바디아 미랜오 미랜오에서 Viridis di Visconti(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1386년 7월 9일 스위스 루즈른 젬파치에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 오스트리아 ka른텐 울프스버그 스트. 폴 임 라반탤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich nasceu em 1 de novembro de 1351, em Wien, Áustria, seu pai, Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Viridis di Visconti em 23 de fevereiro de 1365, em Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Itália. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 9 de julho de 1386, em Sempach, Luzern, Suiça, com 34 anos, e foi sepultado em St. Paul im Lavanttal, Wolfsberg, Kärnten, Áustria.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich родился 1 ноября 1351 г. в местности Вена, Австрия, его отцу по имени Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Viridis di Visconti 23 февраля 1365 г. в местности милано, милано, ломбардя, Италия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 9 июля 1386 г. в местности семпач, лузирн, Швейцария в возрасте 34 лет и был похоронен в местности ст. поул им лавантталь, волфсбирг, кaрнтен, Австрия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Leopold III., der Gerechte, Herzog von Österreich und Innerösterreich nació el 1 de noviembre de 1351, en Wien, Austria, su papá, Albrecht VI., der Weise bzw. der Lahme, Graf von Habsburg, Albrecht II.  als Herzog von Österreich, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Johanna Gräfin von Pfirt, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Viridis di Visconti el 23 de febrero de 1365, en Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Italia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 9 de julio de 1386, en Sempach, Luzern, Suiza, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultado en St. Paul im Lavanttal, Wolfsberg, Kärnten, Austria.
